title: Python: .Net: OpenAI - Support response_format type of json_schema (Structured Outputs)
body:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->
The latest OpenAI models allow passing `json_schema` as the `response_format` `type` property, and supplying the schema itself under the `schema` property. This enforces conformance to the schema by validating output tokens, rather than best LLM effort.

Announced here: https://openai.com/index/introducing-structured-outputs-in-the-api/

This is obviously massively useful to any application using json output, especially when deserialising the json to C# types. 
Please consider adding support for these properties through the SemanticKernel abstractions and .Net client in the short term.

title: .Net: Bug: StreamingChatMessageContent doesn't contain result of tool calls
body:
This issue is a follow-up to the closed issue #12891 

When using streaming responses through `ChatCompletionAgent`, tool calls and their results are not being added to the chat history. This prevents storing and resuming conversations that involve function calling. Information regarding the actual tool call and its arguments can be found, as described in #12891, but the actual results of the tool calls can't.

It turns our that `agent.InvokeStreamingAsync` is indeed returning a `StreamingChatMessageContent` of role `tool`, but without the result of the function. This is equivalent to the non streaming case, but with the exception that this one holds the result of the tool call. It has an item called `FunctionResultContent` with the result. But that item is missing in the `StreamingChatMessageContent`.

From what I can tell it's in https://github.com/microsoft/semantic-kernel/blob/main/dotnet/src/SemanticKernel.Abstractions/AI/ChatClient/ChatResponseUpdateExtensions.cs where we lose the result of the function call. 

So in that method we do get a `ChatResponseUpdate` of role `tool`, that has an `Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionResultContent` in its `Contents` list. However, that item is ignored and the returned `StreamingChatMessageContent` doesn't contain any `Items`. Which is what we're seeing in the program.

So what do you think. Is this a bug or a design choice? It seems like the information is there, but that it's just not being propagated.

It would be awesome to get this fixed, since I'm not sure how to store correct history in-between calls otherwise 🤔 

In general, having access to the same (complete) chat history after a call to either `InvokeAsync` or `InvokeStreamingAsync` would be preferable. 

**Platform**
 - Language: C#
 - Source: 1.63.0
 - AI model: AWS Bedrock

title: .Net: New Feature: Expose GPT-5 Verbosity, ReasonEffort= Minimal parameters to SK
body:
---
name: Feature request
about: Suggest an idea for this project

---

<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->
Since Chat GPT-5 was released, the OpenAI API has two new parameters that can control the AI Response:

1. The `verbosity` parameter (low/medium/high) that controls the length of the answer back
2. The ReasonEffort = Minimal (SK already has high/medium/low, but with GPT-5 a 'minimal' value has also been introduced that SK does not currently support (if you send the value, you get an exception))

It would be nice if SK could support these

title: .Net: Bug: GPT-5 new reasoning level 'minimal' is not working in SK
body:
**Describe the bug**
I tried setting OpenAIPromptExecutionSettings value of 'ReasoningEffort' to the new OpenAI value 'minimal', and I get back **The provided reasoning effort 'minimal' is not supported.**

**To Reproduce**
See above.

**Expected behavior**
To work with 'minimal' reasoning effort with new gpt-5 model. Should also be added to the struct ChatReasoningEffortLevel to have Minimal.

**Screenshots**
---

**Platform**
 - Language: C#
 - Source: SK 1.61.0 
 - AI model: OpenAI: gpt-5
 - IDE: VS
 - OS: Win11

**Additional context**
---

title: Microsoft Guidance integration/implementation
body:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->
Do you plan to integrate your prompt template with the feature implemented in the mirosoft guidance project? https://github.com/microsoft/guidance

Both these project are under the microsoft organization, it would be a pity to waste what you can theoretically achieve with guidance, that's a python native library.
Any plan to do something like that?
I've to say in my project I've currently implemented my own integrations from scratch because C# ecosystem is lackluster in this regard, it has very few low level libraries, but I would be happy to use a library if it implements what I need, and one of these is a prompt template engine like guidance.
Actually I'm experimenting with Fluid/Liquid template but I don't want to reinvent the wheel if there is a chance to reuse something more established and battle tested

Thanks

title: .Net: Bug: Chatcompletion usage object reflects only last LLM call even when multiple has been made
body:
**Describe the bug**
A clear and concise description of what the bug is.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Perform IChatCompletionService.GetChatMessageContentAsync
2. Extract usage object (for example for OpenAI)
3. Check Token count
4. Token count is just last API call, which can be fraction of reality

**Expected behavior**
I expect usage object that sums all OpenAI api calls for proper accounting


**Platform**
 - Language: C#
 - Source: NuGet package 1.54.0
 - AI model: OpenAI:GPT-4o-mini

**Additional context**
To have proper accounting of tokens spend per customer, it is a must to be able to get to proper token count somehow
